mnemonics: Covariance is like a 'Cousin' of Correlation
keyPoints: Covariance measures the linear relationship between two variables
Covariance is calculated as the sum of the product of each pair of values minus the product of the means
Correlation measures the strength and direction of the linear relationship between two variables
Correlation ranges from -1 (perfect negative correlation) to 1 (perfect positive correlation)
Correlation is calculated as the covariance divided by the product of the standard deviations
